tag_descriptions.Device Status:
Query the scanner’s current state without changing any settings. These three read-only commands cover health monitoring, device identification, and regulatory configuration.

| Command | What It Returns |
|---|---|
| `get_status` | Real-time health — battery level, temperature, radio activity, power source, NTP clock sync, and terminal connection |
| `get_version` | Device identity — model, serial number, SKU, and all firmware versions (main, scanner, radio, IoTC) |
| `get_current_region` | Radio regulation — country code, frequency channels, power limits, frequency hopping, and LBT settings |

Use these commands for monitoring dashboards, firmware audits, and compliance checks.
tag_descriptions.Device Configuration:
Read or write the scanner’s entire configuration in a single command — no need to call individual endpoints separately.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `get_config` | Retrieve the full configuration — equivalent to calling get_version, get_status, get_current_region, get_wifi, get_eth, get_endpoint_config, and get_installed_certificate at once |
| `set_config` | Apply configuration changes — Wi-Fi, MQTT endpoint, events, and more in one payload |

Use `get_config` for bulk reads and `set_config` to push settings to one or many scanners.
tag_descriptions.Network Configuration:
Manage the scanner’s Wi-Fi and Ethernet connections. The scanner supports both interfaces simultaneously.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `get_wifi` | Retrieve Wi-Fi status — connected SSID, IP address, signal strength, security type |
| `set_wifi` | Configure Wi-Fi — add or update an SSID, set security (WPA2/WPA3/Enterprise), assign IP settings |
| `delete_wifi_profile` | Remove a saved Wi-Fi profile by SSID |
| `get_eth` | Retrieve Ethernet status — link state, speed, IP address, 802.1X security |

Wi-Fi profiles are stored on the device and reconnect automatically after a reboot.
tag_descriptions.MQTT Endpoint Configuration:
Define where the scanner sends data by configuring the MQTT broker connection.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `config_endpoint` | Set up or update the MQTT broker — URL, port, protocol, credentials, topics, and TLS settings |
| `get_endpoint_config` | Retrieve the current MQTT endpoint configuration |

These commands control the broker URL, publish/subscribe topics, QoS levels, keep-alive interval, and reconnect behavior.
tag_descriptions.Certificate Management:
Install, remove, and inspect TLS/SSL certificates used for secure MQTT and Wi-Fi Enterprise connections.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `install_certificate` | Upload a certificate (CA, client, MQTT, or Wi-Fi) to the scanner |
| `delete_certificate` | Remove an installed certificate by name |
| `get_installed_certificate` | List all certificates currently on the device |

Certificates are required for MQTT-TLS connections and WPA2/WPA3 Enterprise Wi-Fi authentication.
tag_descriptions.System Operations:
Restart the scanner or trigger a firmware update. Both commands interrupt normal operation.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `reboot` | Restart the scanner — applies any pending configuration changes |
| `set_os` | Trigger a firmware update on the device |

`reboot` requires no active inventory. `set_os` requires sufficient battery level.
tag_descriptions.Inventory Control:
Start and stop RFID tag reading (inventory) operations on the scanner.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `control_operation` | Start or stop an RFID inventory scan |

While running, the scanner continuously reads RFID tags and publishes tag data events to the MQTT broker.
tag_descriptions.Operating Mode:
Control how the scanner reads tags — transmit power, session, and RF parameters.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `set_operating_mode` | Set the RFID operating mode — transmit power, session, tag population, and more |
| `get_operating_mode` | Retrieve the current operating mode settings |

These parameters affect read range, scan speed, and how the scanner handles large tag populations.
tag_descriptions.Tag Filtering:
Control which tags are reported after an inventory scan by applying post-read filters.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `set_post_filter` | Define filter rules — match by EPC pattern, memory bank, or mask to include or exclude specific tags |
| `get_post_filter` | Retrieve the current tag filter configuration |

Post-filters reduce noise by only reporting tags that match your criteria.
tag_descriptions.Device Health:
Receive periodic heartbeat events from the scanner to confirm it is alive and connected.

| Event | What It Reports |
|---|---|
| `heartBeatEVT` | Periodic health pulse — battery status, inventory state, and connectivity confirmation |

Configure the heartbeat interval via `set_config` → `eventConfiguration` → `heartbeatConfiguration`. If heartbeats stop arriving, the scanner may be offline.
tag_descriptions.Alerts:
Receive automatic alert events when the scanner crosses a threshold or changes state.

| Event | What It Reports |
|---|---|
| `alerts` | Full alert details — battery, temperature, power, network, firmware, and antenna conditions |
| `alert_short` | Compact alert summary for lightweight monitoring |

Alerts are push-based. Enable or disable specific alert types via `set_config` → `eventConfiguration`.
tag_descriptions.Exceptions:
Receive error events when the scanner encounters an unexpected condition.

| Event | What It Reports |
|---|---|
| `exceptionEVT` | Error details — radio failures, configuration errors, and other exceptional conditions |

Exceptions indicate something went wrong that may require operator attention or automated recovery.
tag_descriptions.MQTT Connectivity:
Track when the scanner connects to or loses connection with the MQTT broker.

| Event | What It Reports |
|---|---|
| `mqttConnEVT` | Broker connection and disconnection events with status details |

Use these events to monitor broker connectivity across your fleet and trigger reconnection alerts.
tag_descriptions.Event Configuration:
Choose which events the scanner publishes to the MQTT broker.

| Command | What It Does |
|---|---|
| `config_events` | Enable or disable specific events — heartbeat, battery, temperature, network, firmware, NTP, and more |

You can also set the heartbeat interval and choose what data to include in each heartbeat.
tag_descriptions.Tag Data Event:
Receive RFID tag reads published by the scanner during an active inventory scan.

| Event | What It Reports |
|---|---|
| `dataEVT` | Tag data — EPC, RSSI, antenna, timestamp, and optional memory bank data for each tag read |

Tag data events stream continuously while inventory is running. Each event contains one or more tag reads with EPC, signal strength, and read metadata.
